text: <par><latinform>kob-gi</latinform> and <latinform>la-do</latinform> were originally split into two different records. The correct form is <latinform>kob-gi la-do</latinform> 'ornament on arm (female)'. Note that 'ornament on arm (male)' is just <latinform>kob-gi</latinform>, so <latinform>la</latinform> may not mean 'arm'.</par>
<par><latinform><*d-lay</latinform> or <latinform><*s-l(y)a</latinform>? or is this from Hindi?</par>
<par>Despite the final dental nasal in Milang <latinform>ɲun-</latinform>, it is assigned to <xref ref="259">#259</xref> because of the doublet with <latinform>ɲuŋ</latinform>.</par>
<par>Milang <latinform>-k</latinform> possibly due to assimilation to following consonant.</par>
<par>The last syll looks like it could be a reflex of 711 *<latinform>t(a/o)ŋ</latinform> WING, or 371 <reconstruction>*g-daŋ</reconstruction> HAIR OF HEAD, or <xref ref="370">#370</xref>.</par>
<par>But compare to Milang term for “male” . The first syllable may be generic, that is, not gender-specific.</par>
<par>Milang <latinform>ɲ-</latinform> looks like a fusion of the nasal prefix plus the root-initial <latinform>y-</latinform>.</par>
<par>PLB or just PL? Given in blue notebook as <latinform>ʔdatᴴ</latinform>, PL.</par>
